response.justification: This is a skilled full-stack engineer with IDE development experience from National Instruments, but the resume reveals minimal Java/Kotlin/JVM expertise and no Spring ecosystem experience, which are core requirements. While the candidate demonstrates strong software craftsmanship and learning ability across multiple languages, a mid-senior position requires deep Java/Spring proficiency from day one, not as a skill to develop on the job.
strengths: IDE development experience: Built components for NI LabWindows/CVI, demonstrating understanding of IDE architecture and developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship: Consistent focus on TDD, clean architecture, and well-designed systems across multiple domains
High-performance systems: Led work on low-latency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests per second
concerns: Minimal Java/Kotlin expertise: Java listed as a skill but no substantial professional projects shown; last Java work mentioned was 8+ years ago at Altom
No Spring ecosystem knowledge: No experience with Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise frameworks that are central to the role
Career trajectory mismatch: Primary focus has been TypeScript/Node, D, C++ rather than Java/JVM development; current role is full-stack TypeScript
